skill.name: sop-code-review
skill.description: A structured SOP for multi-agent pull request review across quality, security, performance, architecture, documentation, and release readiness.
skill.summary: Coordinate a four-phase, multi-agent code review workflow for pull requests.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found the backtick and cryptography alerts are Markdown or keyword false positives, while the workflow does legitimately ask agents to run repository checks such as linting, tests, builds, and integration tests. The skill is publishable with a warning to use sandboxed execution for untrusted repositories.

medium_findings.title: Workflow May Execute Repository Commands
medium_findings.description: The SOP directs agents to run linting, tests, build validation, and integration tests. This is expected for code review, but it can execute scripts from an untrusted repository and should run in a sandbox.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly ask agents to run linting, tests, build checks, and integration tests. The behavior is legitimate for a code review SOP, but project scripts can be unsafe when the reviewed repository is untrusted.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to Markdown javascript fences and template strings in documentation examples. They do not execute a shell by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside SKILL.md documentation code blocks or template literals. No standalone Ruby code or shell backtick execution is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are caused by normal words such as description, descriptive, and design. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged line contains ordinary review language rather than cryptographic code. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, RC4, or similar algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts appear in ordinary review checklist text about database queries, logic correctness, and error handling. No network scanning instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are general code review checklist items. I found no command, endpoint, or instruction for host discovery, port scanning, or network probing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workflow May Execute Repository Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The SOP directs agents to run linting, tests, build validation, and integration tests. This is expected for code review, but it can execute scripts from an untrusted repository and should run in a sandbox.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly ask agents to run linting, tests, build checks, and integration tests. The behavior is legitimate for a code review SOP, but project scripts can be unsafe when the reviewed repository is untrusted.

content.user_title: Review Pull Requests With Multi-Agent SOP
content.value_statement: Large pull requests are hard to review consistently across quality, security, and release risk. This skill provides a staged SOP that assigns specialist reviewers and aggregates approval decisions.
actual_capabilities: Defines a four-phase review process with a four-hour timeline.
Coordinates linting, testing, coverage, and build validation checks.
Assigns specialist reviews for quality, security, performance, architecture, and documentation.
Aggregates findings into blockers, priority suggestions, and optional improvements.
Defines approval, change request, rejection, and author notification actions.
Lists measurable quality gates for tests, coverage, security, and documentation.
limitations: Requires compatible task or agent tooling to execute the workflow.
Does not include runnable scripts or integrations with a specific code hosting platform.
Review quality depends on repository access, available tests, and reviewer model capability.
The four-hour timeline may not fit very small fixes or very large releases.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Pull Request Reviews
use_cases.description: Use one repeatable checklist for quality, security, performance, architecture, and documentation review.

use_cases.title: Prepare High-Risk Changes for Merge
use_cases.description: Coordinate deeper checks before merging changes that affect authentication, data handling, or deployment behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Review Coverage During Delivery
use_cases.description: Assign focused review areas so defects are found before release without relying on one general reviewer.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use sop-code-review to review this pull request. Focus on automated checks, code quality, security, and clear action items.

prompt_templates.title: Review Security-Sensitive Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for sensitive code paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use sop-code-review for this authentication or data handling change. Prioritize security findings, test coverage, rollback risk, and blocker decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Specialist Reviewers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use sop-code-review to coordinate quality, security, performance, architecture, and documentation reviewers. Aggregate findings into blockers and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Final Merge Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use sop-code-review to produce the final merge decision. Include automated check status, specialist review outcomes, unresolved risks, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes the checkout flow and payment validation.
output: Automated checks completed with test and coverage status.
Security review flags input validation and sensitive data handling issues.
Final decision requests changes until blocker findings are resolved.

output_examples.input: Review a small documentation and API client update.
output: Quality and documentation reviewers confirm the change is clear and covered.
Security and performance reviewers find no blocking concerns.
Final recommendation approves the pull request with minor follow-up notes.

output_examples.input: Review a deployment change with database migration impact.
output: Integration and DevOps review focuses on migration safety and rollback planning.
Risk analysis identifies blast radius, monitoring needs, and feature flag guidance.
Final decision is conditional approval after deployment safeguards are confirmed.

best_practices: Run the workflow in a sandbox when reviewing untrusted repositories.
Provide the pull request scope, changed files, test results, and release context.
Resolve blocker and high-severity findings before using the final approval step.
anti_patterns: Do not use the SOP as a substitute for required human approval on critical systems.
Do not run repository scripts from unknown contributors on a privileged machine.
Do not treat every suggested improvement as a merge blocker.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews pull requests across automated checks, code quality, security, performance, architecture, documentation, integration risk, and final approval.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code by itself?
faq.answer: The skill is Markdown guidance, but it instructs agents to run checks such as tests and builds when tool access exists.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use a sandbox for untrusted repositories because test and build scripts can execute project-controlled commands.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It structures review work and surfaces findings, but humans should approve important changes.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect staged review notes, categorized findings, blocker lists, recommendations, and an approval or change request decision.
